taskSummary: Replace placeholder repository review routes with curator-only queue workbenches backed by D1 review APIs and auditable review actions.
assumptions: Static /pages review routes are protected in practice by curator-only APIs and client redirect handling because the current Pages setup does not expose Worker route-permission enforcement for those asset URLs.
Curator review flows are D1-only for this implementation.
issuesAndPivots: Codex review identified that stale queue renewals could preserve an overdue reviewDueAt and leave records immediately due for review again.

Runtime regression testing exposed a second defect where underscore-based action values such as confirm_current were being slug-normalised and rejected. The action parser was updated to preserve underscores.
GOV.UK tabs and pagination were moved into the Nunjucks template, while live counts and page numbers remain runtime-driven by the review API and client controller.

The first tabs pass left inactive queues as placeholder panels. The review template and controller were then revised so all three tabs own real workbench panels and switching tabs updates queue content in place.

That per-tab panel version still produced empty shell panels in practice, so the design was simplified again to use GOV.UK tabs as the switcher with one shared review workbench render surface beneath them.
residualRisks: Static review page shells remain directly addressable as URLs even when the data and actions are blocked.
Repository main page still exposes curator workbench links in static markup.
